visa_types.name: Visas for family members of a citizen of the Union
visa_types.documents_required:
Dear Customers, 
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/aut/travel-insurance).
Please, click here ( https://www.bmeia.gv.at/ru/oeb-astana/reisen-nach-oesterreich/einreise-und-aufenthalt/wie-stelle-ich-einen-antrag/) for more information. 
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and by card and only in KZT currency. 
Kindly check that:
- All required documents are collected and filled correctly;
- The personal e-mail address of the applicant should be indicated correctly, as it is the only way to contact applicant in case of need.
Fees are non-refundable in case of refusal or withdrawal of the application.
A visa does not guarantee the right to enter the country of destination. If reasons emerge that lead to refusal of entry the visa can be annulled.
visa_types.visa_fees:
Visa fee for the application processing is 90 euros (visa fee payment is accepted only in KZT 50 400) Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years –45 euros (payment is accepted in KZT 25 200) Citizens of the countries with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 600).
Consulate fee exempted:
- Children under the 6 years old.
- Family members of citizens of the European Union / European economic space according to the Directive 2004/38/EU.
The more detailed information you can obtain at the Consulate of the Schengen Agreement country, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Application Centre, it is necessary to pay the service fee (including VAT 16%) in amount of 18 170 KZT.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fees are not refundable.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in KZT currency. Also, we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.
visa_types.processing_time:
Application for a visa can be submitted within 6 months before the planned trip. Minimum visa processing time is 15 calendar days. In individual cases the visa processing time can be extended up to 45 calendar days. It is recommended to submit the visa application not later than 15 calendar days, otherwise the Consulate Department has the right to not accept the documents. Holders of multiple entry Schengen (short-term) visas can apply for visa before their visa is expired.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.
visa_types.forms:
A visa application is submitted on an official form “Application for Schengen Visa,” which is provided by the diplomatic mission or can be downloaded here. Form issued by another Schengen state may be used as well. Application form must be filled out completely and legibly. An incomplete
                                                                            or illegible application form will be returned for re-working. The application form must be signed at the last page.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Austria or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Austria/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Austria, unless Austria has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Schengen (short-term) visa issued by a diplomatic mission of Austria or of another Schengen state entitles its holder to stay in the territory of Austria/Schengen area for the period indicated in the visa. Holder of a Schengen (Short-term) visa can stay in a territory of Austria, unless Austria has been excluded from the territorial validity of the visa.
Schengen area is formed by the following states:
Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Iceland, Italy, Lithuania, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Germany, The Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Austria, Greece, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.

## Visa Fees
Visa fee for the application processing is 90 euros (visa fee payment is accepted only in KZT 50 400) Visa fee for children at the age of more than 6 years old and less than 12 years –45 euros (payment is accepted in KZT 25 200) Citizens of the countries with which the EU signed the agreement on simplification of a visa regime pay 35 euros (payment accepts in KZT 19 600).
Consulate fee exempted:
- Children under the 6 years old.
- Family members of citizens of the European Union / European economic space according to the Directive 2004/38/EU.
The more detailed information you can obtain at the Consulate of the Schengen Agreement country, where you would like to submit the visa application form.
If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Application Centre, it is necessary to pay the service fee (including VAT 16%) in amount of 18 170 KZT.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fees are not refundable.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in KZT currency. Also, we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
Dear Customers, 
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/aut/travel-insurance).
Please, click here ( https://www.bmeia.gv.at/ru/oeb-astana/reisen-nach-oesterreich/einreise-und-aufenthalt/wie-stelle-ich-einen-antrag/) for more information. 
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and by card and only in KZT currency. 
Kindly check that:
- All required documents are collected and filled correctly;
- The personal e-mail address of the applicant should be indicated correctly, as it is the only way to contact applicant in case of need.
Fees are non-refundable in case of refusal or withdrawal of the application.
A visa does not guarantee the right to enter the country of destination. If reasons emerge that lead to refusal of entry the visa can be annulled.

## Photo Specifications
 Customers are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below. 
General
- The photograph for each visa Customer must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa Customer submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa customer is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the customer's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                It is preferable that ears be exposed.

## Processing Time
Application for a visa can be submitted within 6 months before the planned trip. Minimum visa processing time is 15 calendar days. In individual cases the visa processing time can be extended up to 45 calendar days. It is recommended to submit the visa application not later than 15 calendar days, otherwise the Consulate Department has the right to not accept the documents. Holders of multiple entry Schengen (short-term) visas can apply for visa before their visa is expired.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
A visa application is submitted on an official form “Application for Schengen Visa,” which is provided by the diplomatic mission or can be downloaded here. Form issued by another Schengen state may be used as well. Application form must be filled out completely and legibly. An incomplete
                                                                            or illegible application form will be returned for re-working. The application form must be signed at the last page.

- Family members of citizens of the European Union / European economic space according to the Directive 2004/38/EU.

If you submit the visa application form through the Visa Application Centre, it is necessary to pay the service fee (including VAT 16%) in amount of 18 170 KZT.
In case of the refusal of visa application the consular and service fees are not refundable.
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and only in KZT currency. Also, we kindly ask you to pay the exact sum of charges.
ATTENTION!
The net banknotes which aren't damaged, not fragmentary, not crumpled, without foreign seals, records, smudges, spots, and punctures are accepted.

## Documents Required
Dear Customers, 
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/kaz/en/aut/travel-insurance).
Please, click here ( https://www.bmeia.gv.at/ru/oeb-astana/reisen-nach-oesterreich/einreise-und-aufenthalt/wie-stelle-ich-einen-antrag/) for more information. 
Payment is accepted in cash desk at the Visa Center only in cash and by card and only in KZT currency. 
Kindly check that:
- All required documents are collected and filled correctly;
- The personal e-mail address of the applicant should be indicated correctly, as it is the only way to contact applicant in case of need.
Fees are non-refundable in case of refusal or withdrawal of the application.
A visa does not guarantee the right to enter the country of destination. If reasons emerge that lead to refusal of entry the visa can be annulled.

## Photo Specifications
 Customers are requested to submit their Application form in the new photo format, as explained below. 
General
- The photograph for each visa Customer must be an un-mounted, full face photo, as displayed above
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa Customer submitted must measure:
- 35 mm * 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure about 3 cms
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa customer is facing the camera directly.
- Face should cover about 70 – 80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted.
- General, the customer's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                                It is preferable that ears be exposed.

## Processing Time
Application for a visa can be submitted within 6 months before the planned trip. Minimum visa processing time is 15 calendar days. In individual cases the visa processing time can be extended up to 45 calendar days. It is recommended to submit the visa application not later than 15 calendar days, otherwise the Consulate Department has the right to not accept the documents. Holders of multiple entry Schengen (short-term) visas can apply for visa before their visa is expired.
Please note that the application processing time and decisions on visa applications are the sole prerogative of the Embassy or Consulate. VFS Global has no role to play in these areas of the application process.

## Download Forms
A visa application is submitted on an official form “Application for Schengen Visa,” which is provided by the diplomatic mission or can be downloaded here. Form issued by another Schengen state may be used as well. Application form must be filled out completely and legibly. An incomplete
                                                                            or illegible application form will be returned for re-working. The application form must be signed at the last page.
